description: Russian criminal organization based in Saint Petersburg, founded in 1988 by Vladimir Kumarin and others from Tambov Oblast. Engaged in protection rackets, drug trafficking, and other illegal activities. Known for conflicts with rival gangs like the Malyshevskaya OGG. Despite arrests, the group remains active.

country_links.evidence_quote: a sector opening in Sydney, Australia allegedly being run by Mikhail Klapanov
country_links.context: The gang has a sector opening in Sydney allegedly run by a member.

country_links.evidence_quote: more than one billion euros of Russian money had been laundered through a series of financial transactions in Bulgaria
country_links.context: The gang launders money through financial transactions in Bulgaria.

country_links.evidence_quote: trafficking of Colombian narcotics through the Leningrad sea port to Europe
country_links.context: The gang traffics Colombian narcotics through the Leningrad sea port to Europe.

country_links.evidence_quote: The rest of the money was transferred to accounts in Cyprus, Dubai, Hong Kong and other countries
country_links.context: The gang transfers laundered money to accounts in Cyprus.

country_links.evidence_quote: more than one billion euros of Russian money had been laundered through a series of financial transactions in Bulgaria and Estonia
country_links.context: The gang launders money through financial transactions in Estonia.

country_links.evidence_quote: very large sums were laundered for them through Latvian and German firms associated with Parex Bank
country_links.context: The gang launders money through German firms associated with Parex Bank.

country_links.evidence_quote: The rest of the money was transferred to accounts in Cyprus, Dubai, Hong Kong and other countries
country_links.context: The gang transfers laundered money to accounts in Hong Kong.

country_links.evidence_quote: Parex Bank, which held mostly offshore deposits for persons from Russia, was the preferred Latvian bank for the money laundering of ... Russian mafia especially the Tambovskaya Mafia
country_links.context: The gang launders money through Parex Bank, a Latvian bank.

country_links.evidence_quote: Spanish police arrested 20 members of the organisation's Spanish branch
country_links.context: The gang has a Spanish branch and members were arrested there for money laundering.

country_links.evidence_quote: the Tamboskaya OGG controls both the Saint Petersburg sea port and the Stockholm sea port
country_links.context: The gang controls the Stockholm sea port.
